To which Public Authority can I file a r: You can file a request to any Central Public Authority which is located in New Delhi, Delhi NCR, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, and Ladakh. If the Public Authority is located in any other State/UT, then the RTI application can be filed through the State/UT RTI Online Portal.
How do I make the payment for RTI fee?: You can make the payment through credit/debit card, net banking or UPI.
What happens if I select a wrong Public: In such cases, the RTI application will be transferred to the correct Public Authority.
Do I get any receipt for online filing o: Yes, an RTI application filed online is assigned a unique registration number and an email is sent to the applicant with the registration number and other details. The applicant can also check the status of his/her RTI application by entering the registration number.
What should I do if I forgot my login cr: In case you have forgotten your username or password, you can click on "Forgot Username/Password" link on the RTI Online Portal and follow the instructions therein to retrieve your username/password.
Can I file RTI application for state pub: No, this portal is only for Central Public Authorities.
How much time does RTI request retain at: The RTI request is retained at this portal for a period of 3 years.
Why is OTP feature introduced in view st: OTP feature is introduced to ensure the security of the requestor's personal information.
What are the reasons for RTI payment fai:
The reasons for RTI payment failing are:

1. Insufficient balance in the bank account.
2. Incorrect bank account details.
3. Internet connectivity issues.
4. Technical issues with the payment gateway.
Do I need to make any payment for filing: No, there is no fee for filing an appeal.
